module_map.responsibility: Entry point for HTTP request processing. BaseHandler loads middleware stack, orchestrates request/response pipeline, manages async/sync boundary crossing, and handles exceptions. WSGIHandler and ASGIHandler adapt WSGI/ASGI entry points.

Request/response processing pipeline. Common middleware provides URL slash redirects and request validation. CSRF middleware provides token-based protection. Cache, security, gzip, and locale middleware add per-request processing hooks for cross-cutting concerns.

URL routing and view resolution. URLResolver matches incoming request paths against URL patterns and dispatches to appropriate views. Pattern matching supports path(), re_path(), include(), and url parameter converters.

HTTP request/response abstraction. HttpRequest wraps WSGI/ASGI environment, parsing GET/POST parameters, cookies, file uploads, and content negotiation. HttpResponse and subclasses (FileResponse, JsonResponse, etc.) represent HTTP responses.

View layer providing View base class and generic views. View.dispatch() routes HTTP method to handler (get, post, etc.). Class-based views integrate with templates, URL reversing, and context management.

ORM and database abstraction layer. Database connections pooled via connections dict. Models define data schema and provide queryset ORM API. Transaction context managers coordinate multi-statement operations.

Template rendering engine supporting template tags, filters, context variables, and inheritance. Loader system supports file-based, app-based, and in-memory templates. Template rendering integrates with context processors.

Centralized configuration management. settings object provides immutable access to all Django settings (DATABASES, INSTALLED_APPS, MIDDLEWARE, etc.). LazySettings ensures thread-safe lazy initialization.
Application registry and initialization. AppConfig defines app metadata and ready() hook for per-app startup code. Apps registry provides central app discovery and model registration.

Signal dispatcher for loose coupling between components. Signals (request_started, request_finished, post_save, pre_delete) allow receivers to subscribe to framework events with sync and async support.

Utility functions and decorators. Provides datastructures (QueryDict, MultiValueDict), functional tools (cached_property, classproperty), logging helpers, deprecation utilities, and text/encoding helpers.

Framework exception hierarchy. Defines domain-specific exceptions: ImproperlyConfigured (config errors), SuspiciousOperation (security events), FieldError (model errors), ValidationError (data validation).
Framework event hooks. request_started and request_finished fire at request lifecycle boundaries. Models emit pre_save, post_save, pre_delete, post_delete for data mutations.

pipeline_trace.description: WSGI/ASGI server calls WSGIHandler or ASGIHandler. Handler.load_middleware() instantiates middleware stack from settings.MIDDLEWARE list. Handler._get_response() or _get_response_async() becomes callable entry point.
Handler wraps WSGI environ dict into HttpRequest. Request parsing deferred: GET/POST parsed on first access, file uploads parsed by MultiPartParser. Request.META contains headers and environ vars.

Middleware chain processes request in order. Each middleware's process_request() hook runs sequentially. CommonMiddleware validates host, handles slash redirects. CsrfViewMiddleware prepares CSRF token. Cache middleware checks for cached response.
Handler calls resolve(request.path) via URLResolver. URLResolver pattern matching returns ResolverMatch with view callable, kwargs, view name. Pattern matching supports regex and path converters.

If view is class-based View, call View.as_view()(*args, **kwargs). View.setup() initializes request/args/kwargs. View.dispatch() routes to HTTP method handler (get, post, put, delete, patch, options, head).

View calls QuerySet methods: filter(), exclude(), get(), all(), annotate(). QuerySet.query builds SQL AST. Executor evaluates query at terminal operation (list(), count(), exists(), first()). Database connection pooled from django.db.connections.

View returns HttpResponse subclass (TemplateResponse, JsonResponse, FileResponse, HttpResponseRedirect). TemplateResponse.render() evaluates templates via django.template.Engine. Response status, headers, cookies populated.

Middleware chain processes response in reverse order. Each middleware's process_response() hook runs. CacheMiddleware caches response. SecurityMiddleware adds security headers. GzipMiddleware compresses body.

If view or middleware raises exception, handler catches and routes to middleware exception handlers. Middleware process_exception() hooks run in reverse. Framework converts exceptions to HttpResponse (404, 500, etc.).
Handler sends HttpResponse to WSGI/ASGI server. request_finished signal emitted. Database connections closed. Response status line, headers, and body serialized per WSGI/ASGI spec.

cross_module_hubs.reason: Central orchestrator of HTTP request/response pipeline. Imports middleware, urls, views, db, signals, exceptions, template, utils. Calls middleware.load_middleware, urls.resolve, views.dispatch, db.transaction, signals.send. Manages async/sync boundary via asgiref.

Core data structure hub. HttpRequest/HttpResponse passed through entire middleware stack, views, and handlers. Imports from datastructures, encoding, files, exceptions. Imported by middleware, views, urls, core.handlers, contrib modules.

Utility library hub. Provides datastructures (QueryDict), decorators (cached_property, MiddlewareMixin), logging (log_response), functional helpers. Imported pervasively by core, middleware, views, db, template, dispatch modules.
Global configuration hub. All modules depend on settings for MIDDLEWARE, DATABASES, INSTALLED_APPS, DEBUG, etc. No outbound dependencies. Accessed synchronously across request/response pipeline.
ORM hub for data access. Views, models, signals, and migrations depend on db module. Transaction context manager coordinates multi-statement operations. Connections pooled and thread-local.

change_proposal.feature_summary: Add async-compatible structured logging middleware that captures per-request metadata (view name, ORM query count, response time) and emits it as structured JSON log entries. Integrates with Python logging and supports async/sync contexts.
new_types: QueryCounter context-local counter (tracks queries per request)
StructuredFormatter log formatter (converts LogRecord to JSON)
change_proposal.pattern_to_follow: Follow existing middleware pattern: inherit from django.utils.deprecation.MiddlewareMixin, implement process_request() and process_response() hooks, support async via async_to_sync/sync_to_async from asgiref. Use django.core.signals (request_started, request_finished) for lifecycle hooks. Store request-local state in django.core.handlers.base RequestMetadata attached to HttpRequest object. Follow django.utils.log patterns for logging configuration.
change_proposal.integration_point: 1. Middleware stack: Add StructuredLoggingMiddleware to django.conf.settings.MIDDLEWARE before CacheMiddleware. 2. Request lifecycle: Hook into request_started signal to initialize RequestMetadata and QueryCounter context. Hook into request_finished signal to finalize metadata and emit structured log. 3. Query instrumentation: Wrap django.db.backends.base.BaseDatabaseWrapper.execute() to increment query counter. 4. View name extraction: Call request.resolver_match.view_name after URL resolution (available after middleware chain reaches view dispatch). 5. Timing: Use time.perf_counter() to measure request duration from request_started to request_finished.
risks: Performance overhead: QueryCounter incremented on every database query; use atomic increment or thread-safe counter. Structured logging JSON serialization may slow down high-traffic sites; consider buffering or async emission.

Async/sync compatibility: Middleware must support both WSGI (sync) and ASGI (async) request contexts. Use asgiref.sync.sync_to_async and async_to_sync carefully; improper usage can block event loop or cause deadlocks.

Request-local state: RequestMetadata stored on HttpRequest object requires care with request object reuse or pooling in async contexts; use contextvars.ContextVar instead of request attributes for robustness.
Circular imports: django.core.handlers imports django.middleware, which imports django.core.handlers; use late imports or restructure to avoid circular dependency.
Database query counting accuracy: Queries issued outside normal ORM path (raw SQL, database drivers) may not be counted. Middleware depends on query hook installed in all database backends.
Backward compatibility: New middleware configuration adds to settings.MIDDLEWARE list; existing deployments must opt-in. New signal handlers may conflict with user signal receivers in edge cases.

Testing complexity: Structured logging middleware requires test fixtures for signal emission, database query mocking, and async request simulation. Integration tests must cover WSGI and ASGI entry points.

tool_usage.params: root: /tmp/benchmark-repos/django, max_depth=2, summary=true - mapped repository structure and top-level packages
path: /tmp/benchmark-repos/django/django, max_depth=1, summary=true - identified core package modules (middleware, urls, views, db, http, etc.)
path: /tmp/benchmark-repos/django/django/middleware, max_depth=1, summary=true - examined middleware stack and individual middleware classes
path: /tmp/benchmark-repos/django/django/urls, max_depth=1, summary=true - analyzed URL routing module structure (resolvers, patterns, converters)
path: /tmp/benchmark-repos/django/django/db, max_depth=1, summary=true - examined ORM and database abstraction
path: /tmp/benchmark-repos/django/django/core, max_depth=1, summary=true - mapped core handlers, signals, exceptions, and utilities
path: /tmp/benchmark-repos/django/django/utils, max_depth=1, summary=true - identified utility library functions and helpers
path: /tmp/benchmark-repos/django/django/core/handlers/base.py - extracted handler functions (load_middleware, _get_response, _get_response_async) and imports
path: /tmp/benchmark-repos/django/django/http/request.py - examined HttpRequest class, headers, QueryDict, and parse functions
path: /tmp/benchmark-repos/django/django/middleware/common.py - analyzed CommonMiddleware patterns and process_request/process_response hooks
